rulings.Case Title: NEAL TREMBATH VS. JACOB MATHIS MD et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 14, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT JACOB MATHIS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment granted. Plaintiff's request for continuance denied. Defendant shifts the burden of proof. Plaintiff's response to special interrogatory number nine states that on April 1, 1996 he learned that the surgical wound was draining two years following the surgery: a granuloma had formed in the surgical wound at the site where defendant had belatedly removed an overlooked suture. On or sometime after April 18, 1997, plaintiff learned that defendant had surgically damaged plaintiff's spine. On or about July 31, 1997, plaintiff learned that there were free-floating bone chips about his spinal cord at the level where defendant had damaged plaintiff's spinal cord. In his reponse to form Interrogatory 6.3, plaintiff describes constant numbness since the surgery. Plaintiff did not file this lawsuit until Decmeber 3, 2004, well beyond the limitations period that commenced, at the latest, July 31, 1997. Plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue of material fact. Plaintiff's declaration not properly before the court because Commissioner Slavit's order of May 20, 2009 precludes plaintiff's testimony. Objection to Plaintiff's declaration sustained. Even, if considered, plaintiff's declaration does not raise a triable issue of fact regarding the statute of limitations or tolling. On July 31, 1997, at the latest, plaintiff had notice or information of cirumstances to put a reasonable person on inquiry. See Jolly vs. Eli Lilly & Co. (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1103. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY DUBAN VS. DANIEL BERKO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 14, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT DANIEL BERKO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment granted. Defendant and cross-complainant shifts the burden of proof. Plaintiff and cross-defendant fails to raise a triable issue of material fact. Defendant's and cross-complainant's requests for admissions deemed admitted in orders dated 9/18/08 and 11/24/08. Plaintiff and cross-defendant has admitted that Berko did not cause him economic harm, the parties have a valid contingency fee agreement, Berko acted with reasonable care in prosecuting plaintiff's case, plaintiff has no evidence he would have obtained more than $200,000.00 and Berko is entitled to at least $119,928.00 in fees. Summary Judgment may be granted based upon admissions deemed admitted. (See Steele vs. Totah (1986) 180 Cal.App.3d 545, 554.) Defendant, cross-complant is entitled to prejudgment interest. (Civil Code section 3287.) The court sustains defendant's and cross-complainant's objections, but even if Duban's declaration is considered, no triable issue of fact is raised. The Court adopts defendant's and cross-complainant's proposed Order granting motion. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ELENA LOKSHINA et al VS. PHIL LYON et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 14, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT PHIL LYON, and, BARBARA LYON DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled. Ten days leave to answer. Plaintiffs plead that they were tenants, which for purposes of demurrer the court accepts as true. Chan vs. Antepenko (1988) 203 Cal.App. 3d Supp.21 is distinquishable. (See Spinks vs. Equity Residential Briarwood Apartments (2009) 171 Cal.App. 4th 1004, 1041-1042.) Factual issues remain whether Rottweiler was brandished in a menacing manner. Plaintiff's do not allege that they formally relinguished possession, but rather removed their belongs in response to plaintiff's demand for possession. Therefore, CC section 789.3 claim proper. =(302/CWW)
